nodes.label: Agricultural Input Cost Surge
nodes.description: Natural gas-derived fertilizer prices spike, threatening food production costs and agricultural margins.
nodes.confidence_note: Natural gas represents 70-90% of ammonia production costs; producers benefit from price increases.

nodes.label: China Helium Export Restrictions
nodes.description: China restricts helium exports as geopolitical leverage during Iran conflict, creating supply shock since China controls ~30% of global helium production and Iran tensions heighten strategic resource competition.
nodes.confidence_note: High likelihood given China's track record of export controls on strategic materials during geopolitical tensions, with direct impact on semiconductor and medical imaging supply chains.

scenarios.label: Contained Regional Conflict
scenarios.market_impact: Oil spikes to $95-100 temporarily before settling back; VIX touches 25-28; defense and energy outperform 2-3 weeks then fade.

scenarios.label: Hormuz Strait Blockade
scenarios.market_impact: Oil surges to $120-140; global recession fears trigger 15-20% equity selloff; Fed forced to choose between inflation and growth.

scenarios.label: Rapid De-escalation
scenarios.market_impact: Relief rally in equities 5-8%; oil gives back war premium falling to $70-75; VIX collapses back to 13-15 range.

scenarios.label: Multi-Front Middle East War
scenarios.market_impact: Systemic crisis with oil at $150+, 25%+ equity drawdown, credit market stress; Fed emergency intervention required.

nodes.label: Semiconductor Fab Production Constrained
nodes.description: Helium is essential for semiconductor manufacturing as a cooling and purging gas in ion implantation and CVD processes, directly throttling wafer output.
nodes.confidence_note: Helium has no practical substitute in chip fab processes; even short supply disruptions force fab utilization cuts of 10-30%.

nodes.label: MRI Scanner Operations Threatened
nodes.description: Liquid helium is the sole coolant for MRI superconducting magnets; shortages force scanner shutdowns and delay elective diagnostics.
nodes.confidence_note: Over 30,000 MRI machines globally rely on liquid helium with no viable alternative coolant at scale.

headline_matches.headline: ‘Healthcare is important to me’: I’m worried about relocating to Florida from New Jersey. Are hospitals there under stress?

nodes.label: Industrial Gas Prices Surge Broadly
nodes.description: Helium shortage tightens the entire industrial gas supply chain, pushing up prices for argon, nitrogen, and specialty gases as buyers scramble for substitutes.
nodes.confidence_note: Air Products and Linde are among the largest helium distributors globally; shortage conditions expand their margin and pricing power.

nodes.label: Defense and Aerospace Programs Delayed
nodes.description: Helium is critical for pressurizing rocket fuel systems, leak detection, and cooling defense electronics, threatening program timelines.
nodes.confidence_note: The US DoD classified helium as a critical mineral; launch schedule slippage directly pressures defense contractor revenue recognition.

nodes.label: Fiber Optic Cable Output Reduced
nodes.description: Helium is used as a cooling gas in the fiber draw process; shortage slows production of optical fiber critical for broadband infrastructure build-out.
nodes.confidence_note: Fiber draw towers require continuous helium flow; production bottlenecks delay ISP and hyperscaler network expansion timelines.

nodes.label: AI Infrastructure Buildout Stalls
nodes.description: Constrained GPU and advanced logic chip supply from fab disruptions delays hyperscaler AI capex deployment, compressing data center revenue growth.
nodes.confidence_note: Hyperscalers have telegraphed $300B+ combined AI capex; any GPU supply constraint directly reprices AI growth narratives.

headline_matches.headline: Eaton finalizes a smart acquisition to advance its AI data center ambitions

nodes.label: Semiconductor Equipment Order Cycles Extend
nodes.description: Fab utilization cuts reduce near-term equipment orders as chipmakers defer tool purchases, pressuring semiconductor capital equipment revenue.
nodes.confidence_note: Equipment names trade at high multiples sensitive to order flow; deferred fab spending creates earnings risk across the tool supply chain.

nodes.label: Diagnostic Delays Raise Hospital Liability
nodes.description: MRI scanner shutdowns extend diagnostic queues, increasing clinical risk and pushing patients toward alternative imaging such as CT, benefiting contrast agent makers.
nodes.confidence_note: GE HealthCare derives significant revenue from MRI service contracts; scanner downtime creates both service revenue risk and parts upsell opportunity.

headline_matches.headline: ‘Healthcare is important to me’: I’m worried about relocating to Florida from New Jersey. Are hospitals there under stress?

nodes.label: Cryogen-Free MRI Technology Investment Accelerates
nodes.description: Shortage urgency accelerates commercial adoption of cryogen-free MRI platforms and closed-loop helium recycling systems, benefiting niche innovators.
nodes.confidence_note: Philips and Siemens have commercially launched reduced-helium MRI lines; shortage accelerates hospital procurement cycles for these systems.

nodes.label: US-Qatar-Russia Helium Supply Geopolitics
nodes.description: With Russia's Amur facility and Qatar's RasGas supplying ~45% of global helium, geopolitical tensions create acute supply concentration risk and sanctions overhang.
nodes.confidence_note: Russia's Gazprom helium exports face sanctions risk; Qatar supply is subject to shipping and diplomatic corridor vulnerability.

nodes.label: Junior Helium Explorers Attract Capital
nodes.description: Price spikes incentivize exploration capex in helium-rich basins in Tanzania, Saskatchewan, and US Rocky Mountain regions, rerating junior miners.
nodes.confidence_note: Helium above $500/Mcf makes previously marginal geological formations economically viable, driving a re-rating of exploration-stage companies.

nodes.label: US Federal Helium Reserve Policy Response
nodes.description: The Bureau of Land Management's Federal Helium Reserve depletion (post-2024 privatization) forces emergency Congressional action to reconstitute strategic stockpiles.
nodes.confidence_note: The BLM sold off the Cliffside Reserve under the Helium Stewardship Act; loss of the government buffer eliminates the traditional price stabilization mechanism.

nodes.label: Helium Cost Feeds Broader PPI Inflation
nodes.description: Higher helium and industrial gas prices flow into PPI for electronics, medical equipment, and manufacturing, adding basis points to sticky goods inflation.
nodes.confidence_note: Helium is a small but non-zero input cost embedded in hundreds of manufacturing processes; aggregate PPI pressure complicates Fed easing timeline.

nodes.label: Supply-Side Inflation Complicates Fed Cuts
nodes.description: Cost-push inflation from commodity shortages gives the Fed cover to delay rate cuts, keeping the front end of the yield curve elevated and strengthening the dollar.
nodes.confidence_note: The Fed has explicitly noted supply-side inflation as non-actionable via rates, but persistent PPI pressure influences the dot-plot median timing.

nodes.label: Tech Earnings Uncertainty Lifts VIX
nodes.description: AI capex delay narrative and chip supply uncertainty increase dispersion in mega-cap tech earnings, elevating implied volatility and the VIX complex.
nodes.confidence_note: Mega-cap tech represents over 30% of S&P 500 weighting; earnings uncertainty in this cohort mechanically drives index-level implied vol higher.

nodes.label: Commercial Space Launch Costs Rise
nodes.description: Helium purge and pressurization costs for liquid-fueled rockets increase, squeezing margins for commercial launch providers and raising satellite deployment costs.
nodes.confidence_note: Rocket Lab and emerging launch providers operate on thin margins; input cost spikes are difficult to pass through in competitive fixed-price launch contracts.

nodes.label: Quantum Computing Milestones Pushed Out
nodes.description: Quantum computers require liquid helium for dilution refrigerators at millikelvin temperatures; shortage delays hardware scaling and commercialization timelines.
nodes.confidence_note: Dilution refrigerators consume significant liquid helium continuously; quantum hardware scaling is directly gated by cryogenic coolant availability.

nodes.label: china semiconductor competitive advantage
nodes.description: China's domestic helium production and strategic reserves give Chinese semiconductor fabs a cost and supply continuity advantage over Western rivals dependent on imported helium for wafer cleaning, fiber optics, and MRI-grade cooling in chip manufacturing.
nodes.confidence_note: China's helium self-sufficiency is still limited but growing, making this advantage real but not yet decisive; geopolitical framing amplifies market sensitivity.

scenarios.label: Acute Shortage — Geopolitical Supply Cutoff
scenarios.market_impact: Severe semiconductor and healthcare disruption triggers a 15-20% drawdown in chip equipment names and AI-exposed mega-caps, with VIX spiking above 30 and industrial gas majors surging 25%+.

scenarios.label: Moderate Chronic Shortage — Structural Tightness
scenarios.market_impact: Helium prices remain elevated for 12-24 months, creating a persistent 5-10% drag on semiconductor fab margins and a durable re-rating of industrial gas producers and helium exploration equities.

scenarios.label: Government Strategic Reserve & Allied Supply Response
scenarios.market_impact: US BLM emergency reserve release combined with accelerated Qatari supply ramp stabilizes prices within 6 months, limiting tech multiple compression to 5% and allowing AI capex to continue largely on schedule.

scenarios.label: Rapid Cryogen-Free & Recycling Technology Adoption
scenarios.market_impact: Accelerated adoption of closed-loop helium recycling and cryogen-free MRI/quantum technologies reduces structural demand by 20% within 3 years, deflating the helium price premium and rewarding healthcare technology innovators.
